title: ctenardisia purpusii
intro: Ctenardisia purpusii, also known as the Mexican cinnamon, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant is valued for its bark, which has medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Ctenardisia purpusii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infections. Ctenardisia purpusii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, syrups, and salves for topical application.
